---
title: "Can You Predict That?"
subtitle: "Unit 4 Task 1"
author: "Sydney Ahlstrom Sanders"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    title-block-banner: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: body
    number-sections: false
    html-math-method: katex
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-copy: hover
    code-tools:
        source: false
        toggle: true
        caption: See code
execute: 
  warning: false
    
---
```{python}
import sys
!{sys.executable} -m pip install scikit-learn polars lets-plot pandas
```
```{python}
import sys
!{sys.executable} -m pip install pyarrow
```
```{python}
import sklearn
import polars as pl
print(f"Sklearn version: {sklearn.__version__}")
print(f"Polars version: {pl.__version__}")
print(f"Python version: {sys.version}")
```

```{python}
import polars as pl
import numpy as np
from lets_plot import *
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
from sklearn import metrics
# add the additional libraries you need to import for ML here
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier

LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)

```


```{python}
# import your data here using polars and the URL
url = "https://github.com/byuidatascience/data4dwellings/raw/master/data-raw/dwellings_ml/dwellings_ml.csv"
dwellings = pl.read_csv(url)

print(dwellings.head())
```

## QUESTION

1. __Take time to get to know your data.__ Based on some initial exploration, identify 3 variables in the dataset you think will have strong predictive ability for determining if a house was built before 1980. Explain/justify the reasoning behind your guesses (for example with plots).

_The three most effective variables I found to use for analysis in my model were one story, living area, and number of bathrooms. I created a few plots to see the differences, and it showed a large concentration of ranch homes built before 1980, and much fewer after that. The living area plot also showed a big increase in living area for homes built after 1980. Older homes also had an average of 2 bathrooms, whereas more modern ones had around 3. Any price category is also a dead giveaway for the year built due to the substantial difference in prices over time, and these categories are what helped me push my model in q2 over 90%._

```{python}
import polars.selectors as cs

analysis = (
    dwellings
    .select(cs.numeric())
    .group_by("before1980")
    .agg(pl.all().mean())
    .transpose(include_header=True, header_name="variable", column_names=["Post_1980", "Pre_1980"])
)

analysis = analysis.with_columns(
    diff = (pl.col("Pre_1980") - pl.col("Post_1980")).abs()
).sort("diff", descending=True)

print("Top Variables by Mean Difference:")
print(analysis.head(10))

df_plot = dwellings.to_pandas()
df_plot['before1980_str'] = df_plot['before1980'].astype(str)
df_plot['one_story_str'] = df_plot['arcstyle_ONE-STORY'].astype(str)

p1 = ggplot(df_plot, aes(x='before1980_str', y='livearea', fill='before1980_str')) + \
    geom_boxplot() + \
    labs(x = "Pre-1980 (1) vs Post-1980 (0)", y = "Living Area", title = "Living Area Distribution")

p2 = ggplot(df_plot, aes(x='yrbuilt', fill='one_story_str')) + \
    geom_density(alpha=0.5) + \
    geom_vline(xintercept=1980, linetype='dashed', color='red') + \
    labs(title = "One-Story Homes Over Time", fill = "Is One-Story?")

p1.show()
p2.show()
```

2. __Build a classification model labeling houses as being built “before 1980” or “during or after 1980”.__ Your goal is to reach or exceed 90% accuracy. Report your final model choice and any other model parameters you may have tweaked (train-test split ratio, tuning parameters, etc).  

_I used a Random Forest classifier model that reached a 92.86% accuracy. The data was prepared by removing the year built and parcel columns to prevent data leakage, and then used an 80/20 train-test split. Increasing the number of estimators and adjusting the max depth improved the performance, as well as a balaced class weight to account for any distribution imbalances between pre and post 1980 homes._

```{python}
X = dwellings.drop(["before1980", "before_1980", "yrbuilt", "parcel"], strict=False)
y = dwellings["before1980"]

X_train, X_test, y_train, y_test = train_test_split(
    X.to_pandas(), 
    y.to_pandas(), 
    test_size=0.20, 
    random_state=42
)

rf = RandomForestClassifier(
    n_estimators=500, 
    max_depth=20, 
    min_samples_split=5,
    random_state=42,
    class_weight='balanced' 
)

rf.fit(X_train, y_train)

y_pred = rf.predict(X_test)
accuracy = metrics.accuracy_score(y_test, y_pred)

print(f"--- Final Model Results ---")
print(f"Model Accuracy: {accuracy:.2%}")
print("\nDetailed Performance Matrix:")
print(metrics.classification_report(y_test, y_pred))
```
